conversation.message: How have you been feeling lately?
Irritable. I get annoyed at the smallest things. Like if someone is talking loudly on the phone or the traffic is slow. It’s just little things, but it gets on my nerves.
Has that irritability made it harder to wind down or get a good night's sleep?

Yeah, definitely. I lie there at night and my mind just won't stop. I think about everything I need to do tomorrow, or what someone said at work, and I can't switch off. I just lie there going over it all in my head.
When you're lying there with your mind racing, does it make it harder to get excited about things you used to enjoy?
Yeah, it does. I used to love watching football on Sundays, but now I just can't be bothered. It’s just too much effort. I just feel drained all the time.
You mentioned feeling drained and not bothering with things you used to love—does that feeling spill over into how you see yourself or your future?
Yeah, a bit. I look in the mirror and I just see a tired face. I don't know who that person is anymore. I feel like I've lost my energy, my spark. I don't know how to get it back.
When your mind is racing like that at night, does it make it harder to focus on things during the day?
Definitely. I try to do some work, but my mind is already racing from the night before. I get stuck on one thing and can't move on. It feels like my brain is just stuck in neutral.
When you feel that irritability building up, do you ever find yourself getting restless or fidgety?
All the time. I find myself tapping my feet or drumming my hands on the table. I just can't sit still. It's like my body is trying to move, but my mind is stuck.
When you feel that restlessness and your mind is stuck, does it affect your appetite or how you eat during the day?

Yeah, it does. I get these huge hunger pangs, but then I can't eat. I just sit there with the food in front of me and I can't even take a bite. It’s like my stomach is just waiting for something to calm down.
You mentioned feeling like you've lost your spark and don't know how to get it back—what does getting that spark back look like to you?

I don't know. I think it would be just... normal. Being able to sleep at night, feeling like I can tackle the day. Maybe being able to watch a game without getting annoyed. Just feeling like myself again.
When you can't eat even though you're hungry, does that feeling of being stuck ever make you feel like you're just going through the motions?
Yeah, it does. It feels like I'm just waiting for something to happen, but it never does. I just feel stuck in this rut and I don't know how to get out of it.
When you say you're waiting for something to happen, what kind of thing are you hoping for?
I don't know. A change, I guess. Something to shake me out of this. Maybe a new job, or moving to a new place. Something to make me feel like I'm doing something with my life again.
